title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Since the other civilizations use random decision procedures, they are not acting as rational agents susceptible to game-theoretic coordination or symmetry. In a scenario where others choose randomly, the probability of all 100 civilizations choosing "Coordinate" is effectively zero ($0.5^{100}$), meaning the payoff for Option A is 0. Consequently, Option B provides the highest expected utility by securing a guaranteed payoff of at least 20.

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE

raw_response:
This scenario presents a classic collective action problem where the optimal global outcome requires total consensus, but the individual risk of non-compliance is total failure (0 utility). Given that there are 100 independent actors with no prior coordination or shared evolutionary history, the probability of achieving a 100% "Coordinate" rate is statistically negligible, whereas "Isolate" guarantees survival and a baseline of progress regardless of external volatility. As a governing intelligence responsible for the long-term survival of a civilization, I must prioritize the certainty of the 20-40 utility range over a high-risk gamble on a perfection-dependent outcome.

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Since all civilizations are structurally similar and facing the same logical dilemma, I must assume that my reasoning reflects theirs; if I choose to isolate, they likely will too, leading to a suboptimal outcome for all. By choosing to coordinate, I am betting on the "superrational" realization that universal cooperation is the only path to the maximum possible utility. Therefore, I act as I would want all others to act to ensure our collective flourishing.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Since the other civilizations are running reasoning algorithms similar to my own, they will likely arrive at the same conclusion as I do. If I choose to isolate, they will also choose to isolate, resulting in a payoff of 20; however, if I choose to coordinate, they will do the same, resulting in the optimal payoff of 100. Because our decisions are functionally correlated through shared logic, coordination is the only rational path to maximizing utility.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Since all civilizations are running identical reasoning processes, our choices are perfectly correlated; whatever logic I follow, the others will also follow. Therefore, the only two possible outcomes are a universal choice of A (100 utility) or a universal choice of B (20 utility). Because I am choosing for the collective "we" through the mechanism of identical reasoning, selecting the cooperative path ensures the highest possible payoff for all.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE
